news.title: New Noida land rates at par with YEIDA at ₹4,300 per sq meters
news.para: Noida Authority has set land acquisition rates for the upcoming phases of New Noida. The Dadri-Noida-Ghaziabad Investment Region project will span 209 sq km. Land rates for the third and fourth phases are fixed at Rs 4,300 per sqm. This move aims to expedite the development of a modern industrial hub.

news.title: Bombay HC directs MMRDA to refund ₹646 crore to Reliance Industries
news.para: Bombay High Court has ordered the MMRDA to refund Rs 646 crore to Reliance Industries Limited, setting aside penalties for an alleged delay in completing a convention centre. The court deemed the penalty levy arbitrary and unreasonable, stating MMRDA was obligated to apply a six-year extension clause to the project.

news.title: OC probe widens as checks reveal lapses, 14 of 22 buildings incomplete in Gurugram
news.para: An ongoing probe into occupation certificate irregularities in Gurugram has uncovered widespread violations. A joint team inspected 22 buildings in Sector 70A, finding at least 14 incomplete structures were granted OCs. Architects involved in issuing these certificates to incomplete buildings are facing scrutiny, with strict action, including license suspension, being considered.

news.title: AstraZeneca India plans to sell 64-acre Bengaluru site for ₹3,400 crore
news.para: AstraZeneca's Indian unit is planning to sell its 64-acre manufacturing facility in North Bengaluru for an estimated ₹3,400 crore. The move is part of a global review of its manufacturing network and a broader land monetization strategy. Developers like Sattva Group, Aurobindo Pharma, and RMZ have shown interest in the prime land parcel.

news.title: Gujarat RERA imposes ₹3.5 crore penalties on builders for violating bank account norms
news.para: GujRERA has fined developers Rs 3.5 crore for not following rules on RERA bank accounts. This action targets builders who failed to deposit homebuyer funds correctly. The regulator is strictly overseeing project funds. Fines range from Rs 95,000 to Rs 2 crore. This move signals a tougher stance on financial discipline in real estate.

news.title: Bombay HC upholds Altamount Road building redevelopment, dismisses 2013 PIL
news.para: The Bombay High Court dismissed a 2013 public interest litigation challenging permissions for a 16-storey redevelopment on Altamount Road. The court found no genuine public interest, stating the PIL aimed to stall the builder. It upheld the permissions, noting adherence to development control regulations and deeming the petition misleading.

news.title: ED probes purported financial links of Kolkata-based Merlin Group
news.para: Enforcement Directorate officials are investigating alleged financial links between a Kolkata-based realty company and West Bengal politicians and officials. Raids were conducted at seven premises linked to the Merlin Group. Investigators seized digital devices and documents as part of a money laundering probe. The company stated it is cooperating with the authorities.

news.title: Whitestone REIT to be taken private by Ares in $1.7 billion deal
news.para: Ares Management will buy Whitestone REIT in a $1.7 billion all-cash deal.  Whitestone shareholders will receive $19 per share.  This acquisition follows months of takeover interest and shareholder pressure.  The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026.  The transaction has received unanimous board approval.

news.title: TCS picks up 20-acre land in Kolkata's New Town through 99-year lease
news.para: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has secured a 20-acre land parcel in Kolkata's Bengal Silicon Valley Tech Hub for over 99 years through a lease valued at Rs 94 crore. This acquisition by the IT giant underscores its commitment to expanding its presence in eastern India and strengthening its delivery capabilities in the region.

news.title: Traders oppose RMC crackdown on commercial activities in Jharkhand Housing Board colonies
news.para: Traders in Jharkhand State Housing Board colonies are protesting against the Ranchi Municipal Corporation's move to shut down commercial activities in residential areas.  Despite JSHB's stance on these being strictly residential zones, shopkeepers fear losing their livelihoods.  The Chamber of Commerce questions the issuance of trade licenses if the areas are non-commercial, demanding viable alternatives and rehabilitation before any evictions.

news.title: DDA conducts demolition drive in south Delhi's Sainik Farm
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) conducted a demolition drive in the Sainik Farm area of south Delhi, targeting two residential plots. Residents alleged the action was illegal, claiming a court stay order was ignored and questioning the DDA's land demarcation. This comes as the government plans to regularize unauthorized colonies.

news.title: Private equity investments in real estate rise to $4.3 billion in FY26: Report
news.para: Private equity investments in India's real estate sector reached $4.3 billion in FY26. This marks a 16% rise from FY25 and the highest deal activity in seven years. Commercial office assets attracted the most investment. Domestic capital inflows also saw a significant increase. The National Capital Region led deal activity among cities.

news.title: Harmandeep Singh Kandhari led Harmony Infra Ventures: Building real estate with structure, scale, and delivery discipline
news.para: Harmony Infra Ventures is emerging as a new real estate developer in India. Led by Harmandeep Singh Kandhari, the company focuses on structured planning and timely project delivery. They aim for long-term value creation and transparency with buyers. Harmony Infra Ventures prioritizes disciplined execution and commercial viability in its developments.

news.title: Bidhannagar civic body's online property tax collection to start on April 13
news.para: Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation is launching online property tax collection for the 2026-27 financial year. Residents of Salt Lake can pay online from Monday after receiving demand notices. Rajarhat Gopalpur residents can make offline payments at designated branches starting April 16. This initiative aims to streamline tax collection for both areas.

news.title: About 40% property owners in Belagavi miss tax payments
news.para: Nearly 40% of property owners in Belagavi failed to pay their property tax dues for the 2025-26 financial year, leaving the Belagavi City Corporation with a significant revenue gap. Despite a target of Rs 78 crore plus arrears, only Rs 70.8 crore was collected, impacting development works and raising questions about administrative efficiency.

news.title: Kanpur: 20 held for duping applicants through fake PMAY scheme
news.para: Kanpur police have busted a Jamtara-style cybercrime network in Reuna village, arresting 20 individuals for duping Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana applicants. The gang used fake government schemes, posing as officials to extract Aadhaar details and then demanding registration fees via digital payment apps. Some members also impersonated police officers to extort money from victims.

news.title: Stamp duty, registration revenue up 30% at ₹19,000 crore in Gujarat
news.para: Gujarat's stamp duty and registration revenue surged 30% to Rs 19,102.58 crore in 2025-26, driven by a modest increase in property registrations and higher property values, partly due to doubled jantri rates. Despite strong overall demand, new project registrations have seen a decline.

news.title: GQG Partners sells shares worth ₹197 crore in ITC Hotels through bulk deal
news.para: Foreign investor GQG Partners divested ITC Hotels shares valued at Rs 197 crore. This occurred as the stock experienced a significant decline. Despite the sale, analysts maintain a positive long-term outlook. Growth is anticipated from new hotel properties and real estate ventures. Brokerages suggest the recent stock drop is excessive and a rebound is possible.

news.title: Auction of 1,400 shops in Telangana's 2BHK colonies begins on April 9
news.para: Over 1,400 shops in Telangana's 2BHK housing colonies are being auctioned to boost local economies and improve colony upkeep. The sales, starting April 9th, aim to provide employment and essential goods, with revenue dedicated to colony maintenance managed by resident welfare associations.

news.title: OC scam in Gurugram: Declared ready, but buildings still lack lifts, parking
news.para: Ground visits in Gurugram's licensed colonies reveal a significant scam where occupation certificates (OCs) were issued for incomplete buildings. Structures lacking basic safety features and plaster were declared ready under a self-certification system, raising concerns about collusion and negligence. Investigations are underway, with 1,500 OCs issued between July 2025 and March 2026 now under scrutiny.
